contents.value:
```xml
<person>
  <name>John</name>
  <age>30</age>
</person>
```
**Elements** are the building blocks of XML. They contain text, attributes, and/or child elements. Tags are case-sensitive.

```xml
<item id="123" type="book">
```
**Attributes** provide metadata about elements. Values must always be quoted (single or double). Attribute names must be unique per element.

```xml
<root xmlns:app="http://example.com/app">
  <app:config>value</app:config>
</root>
```
**Namespaces** qualify element/attribute names with a URI to avoid conflicts between different XML vocabularies.

```xml
<!-- This is a comment -->
<!-- TODO: fix this section -->
```
**Comments** are ignored by XML parsers. Cannot contain `--` within the comment body.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
```
**XML declaration** specifies version and encoding. Must appear at the very beginning of the document (before any whitespace).

```xpath
/root/child          select child elements
//element            select anywhere in doc
.//node[@attr]       relative with predicate
text()               get text content
```
**XPath** is a query language for navigating XML documents and selecting nodes.

```xml
<    <
>    >
&   &
"  "
'  '
```
**Predefined entities** represent special characters that cannot appear literally in XML content.
